vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. The pots are present, but the biological specimens expected (P1-P4) are not visible or are in a state of total senescence/decomposition.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss: No foliage detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos is absent.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit figurine present, but host plant is missing.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and potentially anaerobic. No fungal blooms visible, but soil appears heavily saturated.
biome_observations.debris: White rabbit figurine (P3) is the only non-soil object. No organic growth or incidental weeds detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, empty environment. There is no growth, no recovery, and no change in the state of the soil or the presence of the plants over the 5-day period.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered plants have suffered total systemic loss. The environment is essentially a collection of empty pots containing damp soil.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit figurine in an empty pot (P3) is the only non-botanical feature. No new plant introductions have occurred.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline registry and compare it against the visual data. Upon validation, it is clear that the registry does not match the physical reality of the pots. Each pot is devoid of the expected botanical specimens. The soil appears consistently dark and damp across all frames, suggesting a lack of drainage or an over-saturation issue that likely contributed to the total loss of the plants. No new growth has occurred during the observation window.
